subject: Geology
title: What Are Sedimentary Rocks?
description: One-minute skill: What Are Sedimentary Rocks?.

hook.text: Every grain of sand on the beach could become rock. Squeeze it, cement it, and wait a few million years.

buildup.text: Sedimentary rocks form from accumulated sediment, compacted and cemented over time through lithification.

discovery.text: They cover about 75% of Earth's land surface, forming the visible skin even though they're only 5% of the crust.

twist.text: Sedimentary rocks are the only type that contains fossils. Every dinosaur bone was preserved in sedimentary stone.

climax.text: They record Earth's surface history: ancient seas, deserts, rivers, and swamps, all preserved in layered stone.

quiz.question: Why are sedimentary rocks important for paleontology?
options: They are the only rock type that contains fossils
